Geopolitical conflict remains high with reports of "Israeli settlers burn tents, vehicles in West Bank village" and the "cartel war breaks out" in Mexico, indicating widespread instability. Trade stress is exacerbated by protectionist measures like the "US tariff rate to hit 15% or more for some nations" and the formation of regional blocs, as highlighted by the "EU's E6 Power Bloc." While the "U.S. Eases Restrictions on Sale of Venezuelan Oil to Cuba" introduces some policy flexibility, it does not broadly alleviate the trend of rising regulatory and trade fragmentation impacting the global risk profile.
